title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds bounded page-resource and timing audit from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded page-resource and timing audit while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Separate queue, DNS, connect, TLS, request, first-byte, body, parse, render, and total timing with clock and instrumentation limits.
Classify document, stylesheet, script, image, font, media, API, iframe, preload, and redirect resources with initiator and origin.
Reconcile compressed transfer, decoded body, cached reuse, duplicate URLs, variant keys, and large-resource policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Response Timing Phases
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 7989 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate crawler timings, resource graphs, transfer, cache evidence, controlled browser evidence, and user-performance limits; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop User Performance Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-user-performance-boundary-1p2znu" that uses case 7525 to separate crawler timings, resource graphs, transfer, cache evidence, controlled browser evidence, and user-performance limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop User Performance Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns User Performance Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop User Performance Boundary, keep User Performance Boundary connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-user-performance-boundary-1p2znu
# Competency: scraper-user-performance-boundary.
# Case 7525: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_user_performance_boundary_1p2znu(pages: int = 12, resources: int = 180, transfer_bytes: int = 2400000, expanded_bytes: int = 6700000, user_experience_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1p2znu = "1p2znu-unz2p1-7525"
    assert evidence_variant_1p2znu
    evidence_axes_1p2znu = (
        "authorization1p2znu",
        "url7525",
        "responseunz2p1",
        "tree1p2znu7525",
        "frontier75251p2znu",
        "reportunz2p17525",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1p2znu)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or resources < pages:
        return False, resources, "resource-count"
    if transfer_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < transfer_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "transfer-accounting"
    if user_experience_claims > 0:
        return False, user_experience_claims, "user-performance-overclaim"
    if resources > 1000:
        return False, resources, "resource-budget"
    assert expanded_bytes >= transfer_bytes and user_experience_claims == 0
    return True, resources, "bounded-resource-audit"
